2604.11742.title: Discourse Diversity in Multi-Turn Empathic Dialogue
2604.11742.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) produce responses rated as highly empathic in single-turn settings (Ayers et al., 2023; Lee et al., 2024), yet they are also known to be formulaic generators that reuse the same lexical patterns, syntactic templates, and discourse structures across tasks (Jiang et al., 2025; Shaib et al., 2024; Namuduri et al., 2025). Less attention has been paid to whether this formulaicity extends to the level of discourse moves, i.e., what a response does for the person it is addressing. This question is especially consequential for empathic dialogue, where effective support demands not just a kind response at one moment but varied strategies as a conversation unfolds (Stiles et al., 1998). Indeed, prior work shows that LLMs reuse the same tactic sequences more than human supporters in single-turn settings (Gueorguieva et al., 2026). We extend this analysis to multi-turn conversations and find that the rigidity compounds: once a tactic appears in a supporter turn, LLMs reuse it in the next at nearly double the rate of humans (0.50-0.56 vs. 0.27). This pattern holds across LLMs serving as supporters in real emotional support conversations, and is invisible to standard similarity metrics. To address this gap, we introduce MINT (Multi-turn Inter-tactic Novelty Training), the first reinforcement learning framework to optimize discourse move diversity across multi-turn empathic dialogue. The best MINT variant combines an empathy quality reward with a cross-turn tactic novelty signal, improving aggregate empathy by 25.3% over vanilla across 1.7B and 4B models while reducing cross-turn discourse move repetition by 26.3% on the 4B model, surpassing all baselines including quality-only and token-level diversity methods on both measures. These results suggest that what current models lack is not empathy itself, but the ability to vary their discourse moves across a conversation.
2604.11742.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在單輪對話中產生的回應被評價為高度具同理心（Ayers et al., 2023; Lee et al., 2024），但它們也被認為是公式化的生成器，會在不同任務中重複使用相同的詞彙模式、句法模板和話語結構（Jiang et al., 2025; Shaib et al., 2024; Namuduri et al., 2025）。對於這種公式化是否延伸到話語行為的層面，即回應對所面對的人所起的作用，關注較少。這一問題對於具同理心的對話尤為重要，因為有效的支持不僅需要在某一時刻給出友善的回應，還需要隨著對話的展開採用多樣的策略（Stiles et al., 1998）。事實上，先前的研究顯示，在單輪對話中，LLMs重複使用相同的策略序列的頻率高於人類支持者（Gueorguieva et al., 2026）。我們將這一分析擴展到多輪對話，發現這種僵化情況加劇：一旦某個策略出現在支持者的回合中，LLMs在下一回合中重複使用的頻率幾乎是人類的兩倍（0.50-0.56 vs. 0.27）。這一模式在LLMs作為真實情感支持對話中的支持者時依然存在，且對於標準的相似性度量來說是不可見的。為了解決這一空白，我們引入了MINT（多輪內部策略新穎性訓練），這是第一個強化學習框架，旨在優化多輪具同理心對話中的話語行為多樣性。最佳的MINT變體結合了同理心質量獎勵和跨回合策略新穎性信號，使得在1.7B和4B模型上，總體同理心提高了25.3%，同時在4B模型上減少了26.3%的跨回合話語行為重複，超越了所有基準，包括僅質量和基於標記的多樣性方法。這些結果表明，當前模型所缺乏的不是同理心本身，而是能夠在對話中變化話語行為的能力。
2604.11742.author: Hongli Zhan et.al.
2604.11742.authors: Hongli Zhan, Emma S. Gueorguieva, Javier Hernandez, Jina Suh, Desmond C. Ong, Junyi Jessy Li
